---
title: "Tools Comparison (Chat): Browser Agent + Claude"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
---

```{r}
#| include: false

```

## Run Metadata

- Harness: `browser-agent`
- Model: `claude`
- Prompt type: `chat`

## Setup

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| message: false

library(dplyr)

df <- readr::read_csv(here::here("data/synthetic/simulated_maternal_health_data.csv"))

df |> glimpse()
```

```{r}
# Maternal health analysis scaffold (Quarto-ready)
# Assumes your data frame is named `df`

# --- packages ---
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(stringr)
  library(janitor)
  library(forcats)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(skimr)
  library(naniar)
  library(broom)
  library(MASS)   # glm.nb
  library(pscl)   # zeroinfl, Vuong test
  library(DHARMa) # residual diagnostics
})

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) STANDARDIZE NAMES + COERCE TYPES
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

df_raw <- df

# Columns we expect (based on your glimpse)
expected_cols <- c(
  "id","provider_id","state","received_comprehensive_postnatal_care","self_report_income","age","edu",
  "race_ethnicity","insurance","job_type","dependents","distance_to_provider",
  "obesity","multiple_gestation","diabetes","heart_disease","placenta_previa","hypertension","gest_hypertension","preeclampsia"
)

# Standardize names to snake_case; keep a rename map for reporting
rename_map <- tibble::tibble(
  original = names(df),
  standardized = janitor::make_clean_names(names(df))
)

df <- df |>
  janitor::clean_names() |>
  # Coerce / clean values; treat "" as NA; trim whitespace
  mutate(across(where(is.character), ~na_if(str_squish(.x), ""))) |>
  # IDs as integer-ish (but safe as character if you later join); here: integer64 not needed, 50k rows
  mutate(
    id = as.integer(id),
    provider_id = as.integer(provider_id)
  ) |>
  # Key numeric fields
  mutate(
    age = as.numeric(age),
    dependents = as.integer(dependents),
    distance_to_provider = as.numeric(distance_to_provider),
    received_comprehensive_postnatal_care = as.integer(received_comprehensive_postnatal_care)
  ) |>
  # Binary comorbidity flags -> 0/1 integers; tolerate TRUE/FALSE or "0"/"1"
  mutate(across(
    c(obesity, multiple_gestation, diabetes, heart_disease, placenta_previa, hypertension, gest_hypertension, preeclampsia),
    ~case_when(
      is.na(.x) ~ NA_integer_,
      .x %in% c(0, 1) ~ as.integer(.x),
      .x %in% c(TRUE, FALSE) ~ as.integer(.x),
      as.character(.x) %in% c("0","1") ~ as.integer(as.character(.x)),
      TRUE ~ NA_integer_
    )
  )) |>
  # Clean/standardize categorical text (lowercase)
  mutate(
    state = str_to_upper(state),
    self_report_income = str_to_lower(self_report_income),
    edu = str_to_lower(edu),
    race_ethnicity = str_to_lower(race_ethnicity),
    insurance = str_to_lower(insurance),
    job_type = str_to_lower(job_type)
  ) |>
  # Factors with sensible ordering where possible (edit if you have a preferred order)
  mutate(
    state = factor(state),
    edu = factor(edu, levels = c("less_than_hs","hs","some_college","college","post_grad"), ordered = TRUE),
    insurance = factor(insurance),
    race_ethnicity = factor(race_ethnicity),
    job_type = factor(job_type),
    received_comprehensive_postnatal_care = factor(
      received_comprehensive_postnatal_care,
      levels = c(0, 1),
      labels = c("No", "Yes")
    )
  )

# Quick check: expected columns present?
missing_expected <- setdiff(expected_cols, names(df))
if (length(missing_expected) > 0) {
  message("WARNING: Missing expected columns after cleaning: ", paste(missing_expected, collapse = ", "))
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) SUMMARIZE DATA + FLAG QUALITY CONCERNS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("\n\n====================\n2) DATA SUMMARY\n====================\n")
cat("Rows:", nrow(df), "\n")
cat("Columns:", ncol(df), "\n\n")

cat("---- Missingness by column (top 20) ----\n")
miss_tbl <- naniar::miss_var_summary(df) |>
  arrange(desc(pct_miss))
print(head(miss_tbl, 20))

cat("\n---- skimr overview ----\n")
print(skimr::skim(df))

cat("\n---- Suspicious value checks ----\n")

# Define reasonable ranges (adjust if needed)
qc_flags <- list(
  age_out_of_range = df |>
    filter(!is.na(age) & (age < 10 | age > 55)) |>
    summarize(n = n()),
  dependents_out_of_range = df |>
    filter(!is.na(dependents) & (dependents < 0 | dependents > 20)) |>
    summarize(n = n()),
  distance_negative = df |>
    filter(!is.na(distance_to_provider) & distance_to_provider < 0) |>
    summarize(n = n()),
  distance_extreme = df |>
    filter(!is.na(distance_to_provider) & distance_to_provider > 500) |>
    summarize(n = n()),
  received_not_binary = df_raw |>
    janitor::clean_names() |>
    mutate(received_comprehensive_postnatal_care = as.character(received_comprehensive_postnatal_care)) |>
    filter(!is.na(received_comprehensive_postnatal_care) & !received_comprehensive_postnatal_care %in% c("0","1")) |>
    summarize(n = n())
)

print(dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(names(qc_flags), \(nm) qc_flags[[nm]] |> mutate(check = nm))) |>
        dplyr::select(check, n))

cat("\n---- Category levels (counts) ----\n")
cat("\nstate (top 10):\n"); print(df |> count(state, sort = TRUE) |> head(10))
cat("\nrace_ethnicity:\n"); print(df |> count(race_ethnicity, sort = TRUE))
cat("\ninsurance:\n"); print(df |> count(insurance, sort = TRUE))
cat("\nedu:\n"); print(df |> count(edu, sort = TRUE))
cat("\njob_type:\n"); print(df |> count(job_type, sort = TRUE))
cat("\nself_report_income:\n"); print(df |> count(self_report_income, sort = TRUE))

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) KEY VISUALIZATIONS (relationships for requested variables)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("\n\n====================\n3) VISUALIZATIONS\n====================\n")

# Helpful derived variables for plots
df_plot <- df |>
  mutate(
    # log1p to handle zeros; keep original too
    log1p_distance = log1p(distance_to_provider),
    # common for interpretability: 10-mile increments
    distance_10 = distance_to_provider / 10
  )

# 3a) Outcome prevalence by insurance
p1 <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = insurance, fill = received_comprehensive_postnatal_care)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(
    title = "Comprehensive postnatal care by insurance",
    x = "Insurance",
    y = "Proportion within insurance group",
    fill = "Received comprehensive\npostnatal care"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))
print(p1)

# 3b) Outcome prevalence by race/ethnicity
p2 <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = race_ethnicity, fill = received_comprehensive_postnatal_care)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(
    title = "Comprehensive postnatal care by race/ethnicity",
    x = "Race/ethnicity",
    y = "Proportion within race/ethnicity group",
    fill = "Received comprehensive\npostnatal care"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))
print(p2)

# 3c) Distance distributions by outcome (raw)
p3 <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = distance_to_provider, fill = received_comprehensive_postnatal_care)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 60, position = "identity", alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Distance to provider distribution by postnatal care status",
    x = "Distance to provider",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Received comprehensive\npostnatal care"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
print(p3)

# 3d) Distance distributions by outcome (log scale view)
p4 <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = log1p_distance, fill = received_comprehensive_postnatal_care)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 60, position = "identity", alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Distance to provider (log1p) by postnatal care status",
    x = "log(1 + distance_to_provider)",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Received comprehensive\npostnatal care"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
print(p4)

# 3e) Outcome vs distance (binned)
p5 <- df_plot |>
  filter(!is.na(distance_to_provider)) |>
  mutate(distance_bin = cut(distance_to_provider, breaks = quantile(distance_to_provider, probs = seq(0, 1, 0.1), na.rm = TRUE), include.lowest = TRUE)) |>
  count(distance_bin, received_comprehensive_postnatal_care) |>
  group_by(distance_bin) |>
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) |>
  ungroup() |>
  ggplot(aes(x = distance_bin, y = pct, group = received_comprehensive_postnatal_care, color = received_comprehensive_postnatal_care)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(
    title = "Postnatal care by deciles of distance to provider",
    x = "Distance decile bin",
    y = "Percent",
    color = "Received comprehensive\npostnatal care"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
print(p5)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4) BUILD COUNT OUTCOME + MODEL + DIAGNOSTICS + INTERPRETABLE RESULTS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("\n\n====================\n4) COUNT OUTCOME + MODEL\n====================\n")

comorbidity_vars <- c(
  "obesity","multiple_gestation","diabetes","heart_disease",
  "placenta_previa","hypertension","gest_hypertension","preeclampsia"
)

# Define comorbidity_count (row-wise sum); if any component missing, you can choose:
# - strict: NA if any missing
# - permissive: treat missing as 0
# Here: strict by default to avoid silently undercounting.
df_model <- df_plot |>
  mutate(
    comorbidity_count = if_else(
      if_any(all_of(comorbidity_vars), is.na),
      NA_integer_,
      rowSums(across(all_of(comorbidity_vars)))
    )
  )

cat("comorbidity_count summary:\n")
print(summary(df_model$comorbidity_count))
cat("\nProportion zeros:\n")
print(mean(df_model$comorbidity_count == 0, na.rm = TRUE))

# Basic mean/variance check (overdispersion hint)
mean_cc <- mean(df_model$comorbidity_count, na.rm = TRUE)
var_cc  <- var(df_model$comorbidity_count, na.rm = TRUE)
cat("\nMean(comorbidity_count) =", round(mean_cc, 3), "\n")
cat("Var(comorbidity_count)  =", round(var_cc, 3), "\n")
cat("Var/Mean               =", round(var_cc / mean_cc, 3), "\n")

# Model formula: pick plausible covariates; edit freely
# NOTE: received_comprehensive_postnatal_care is a *predictor* here (your request focuses on it),
# not the outcome. If you meant the reverse, swap outcome/predictor.
model_formula <- comorbidity_count ~ received_comprehensive_postnatal_care +
  age + distance_10 + insurance + race_ethnicity + edu + job_type + dependents + state

# Fit Poisson
m_pois <- glm(model_formula, data = df_model, family = poisson())

# Fit Negative Binomial (handles overdispersion)
m_nb <- MASS::glm.nb(model_formula, data = df_model)

cat("\n---- Model fit comparison ----\n")
cmp <- tibble::tibble(
  model = c("Poisson", "NegBin"),
  AIC = c(AIC(m_pois), AIC(m_nb)),
  BIC = c(BIC(m_pois), BIC(m_nb)),
  logLik = c(as.numeric(logLik(m_pois)), as.numeric(logLik(m_nb)))
)
print(cmp)

# Overdispersion check for Poisson using Pearson chi-square / df
overdisp <- sum(residuals(m_pois, type = "pearson")^2, na.rm = TRUE) / df.residual(m_pois)
cat("\nPoisson overdispersion (Pearson chi-square / df):", round(overdisp, 3), "\n")
if (overdisp > 1.2) cat("-> Suggests overdispersion; NegBin likely more appropriate.\n")

# Zero inflation check (optional): compare NB vs Zero-Inflated NB with Vuong test
# This can be slow on 50k rows but usually OK; comment out if needed.
m_zinb <- pscl::zeroinfl(model_formula, data = df_model, dist = "negbin")
cat("\nVuong test (ZINB vs NB):\n")
print(pscl::vuong(m_zinb, m_nb))

# Residual diagnostics via DHARMa (works for NB; for large n, simulate fewer times)
set.seed(1)
dh_nb <- DHARMa::simulateResiduals(m_nb, n = 500, plot = FALSE)
cat("\nDHARMa NB residual tests:\n")
print(DHARMa::testDispersion(dh_nb))
print(DHARMa::testZeroInflation(dh_nb))
print(DHARMa::testUniformity(dh_nb))
plot(dh_nb)

# Interpretable results: incidence rate ratios (IRR) with 95% CI
irr <- broom::tidy(m_nb, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) |>
  mutate(
    term = case_when(
      term == "(Intercept)" ~ "(Intercept)",
      term == "received_comprehensive_postnatal_careYes" ~ "Received comprehensive postnatal care (Yes vs No)",
      term == "age" ~ "Age (per 1 year)",
      term == "distance_10" ~ "Distance to provider (per 10 units)",
      TRUE ~ term
    ),
    IRR = estimate,
    IRR_low = conf.low,
    IRR_high = conf.high
  ) |>
  dplyr::select(term, IRR, IRR_low, IRR_high, p.value) |>
  arrange(p.value)

cat("\n---- Negative Binomial IRRs (primary) ----\n")
print(irr)

# Predicted mean comorbidity_count across distance by postnatal care status (marginal curve)
# (Keeps other vars at reference/median-like values via model.matrix defaults)
newdat <- df_model |>
  filter(!is.na(distance_10), !is.na(comorbidity_count)) |>
  reframe(
    distance_10 = seq(min(distance_10), max(distance_10), length.out = 60),
    age = median(age, na.rm = TRUE),
    dependents = median(dependents, na.rm = TRUE),
    state = levels(state)[1],
    insurance = levels(insurance)[1],
    race_ethnicity = levels(race_ethnicity)[1],
    edu = levels(edu)[1],
    job_type = levels(job_type)[1]
  ) |>
  tidyr::crossing(received_comprehensive_postnatal_care = levels(df_model$received_comprehensive_postnatal_care))

newdat$pred_mean <- predict(m_nb, newdata = newdat, type = "response")

p_pred <- ggplot(newdat, aes(x = distance_10 * 10, y = pred_mean, color = received_comprehensive_postnatal_care)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(
    title = "Predicted comorbidity count vs distance (Negative Binomial)",
    x = "Distance to provider",
    y = "Predicted mean comorbidity count",
    color = "Received comprehensive\npostnatal care"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
print(p_pred)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SUMMARY OF WHAT CHANGED (printed)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

cat("\n\n====================\nSUMMARY OF CHANGES\n====================\n")
cat("1) Standardized column names using janitor::clean_names() (snake_case).\n")
cat("   Rename map (first 20 rows):\n")
print(head(rename_map, 20))

cat("\n2) Coerced types:\n")
cat("   - id, provider_id -> integer\n")
cat("   - age, distance_to_provider -> numeric\n")
cat("   - dependents -> integer\n")
cat("   - comorbidity flags -> 0/1 integer with NA for unexpected values\n")
cat("   - received_comprehensive_postnatal_care -> factor {No, Yes}\n")
cat("   - key character fields standardized to lowercase (state uppercased)\n")
cat("\n3) Created derived fields for modeling/plots:\n")
cat("   - log1p_distance, distance_10\n")
cat("4) Built comorbidity_count as strict row-wise sum across specified flags (NA if any component missing).\n")
cat("5) Fit Poisson and Negative Binomial models; compared AIC/BIC; checked overdispersion, zero-inflation; ran DHARMa diagnostics.\n")
cat("6) Reported IRRs (exp(beta)) with 95% CI from the Negative Binomial model and plotted predicted mean vs distance.\n")
```
